contents:
# Reports Emerge of Iranian Strikes Destroying Over 100 U.S. Military Bases in Retaliation

## Overview
Iran launched coordinated missile and drone strikes targeting over 100 U.S. military bases, causing widespread damage to runways, radar systems, and storage depots. Early estimates indicate billions in financial impact, prompting Pentagon scrutiny of defense preparedness. U.S. officials acknowledge some damage but remain reviewing the full extent.[1]

## Details
In the ongoing 2026 Iran-U.S. conflict, Iran conducted a massive retaliation campaign against U.S. military infrastructure, hitting over 100 targets with missiles and drones. Damage includes key facilities like runways and radar systems, exposing potential vulnerabilities in U.S. defenses. Pentagon sources remain questioning the scale of losses and reporting transparency amid escalating tensions. This follows a ceasefire announced on April 7, 2026, after 40 days of war, during which the U.S. depleted significant stockpiles, including over 850 Tomahawk missiles—representing over 30% of its inventory. The strikes occurred amid U.S. Operation Epic Fury and a tightened maritime blockade of Iran. Current assessments remain preliminary as the U.S. evaluates impacts.[1][3]

## Location
U.S. military bases across the Gulf region

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @AuroraIntel (Aurora Intel). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.
